steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the best description of the text's overall structure. Map the passage in order: (1) a study is introduced with survey data from two cities, (2) the results are stated (53.5% vs. 44.7%), (3) a possible explanation is raised (nonpark amenities), and (4) that explanation is rejected.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'An unexpected finding...attributed to the method used to collect survey data': The passage never questions or credits the data-collection method. ✗
  • Choice B — 'An unresolved question about park planning...answered': The passage opens with a study description, not an unresolved question. ✗
  • Choice C — 'A study...is introduced, and then a possible explanation...is considered and rejected': Matches the passage exactly — study introduced, results given, nonpark-amenity explanation raised and then refuted. ✓
  • Choice D — 'A study of Panama City...a similar study of Caracas...corroborate': Both cities are part of one single study; no second study corroborates the first. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
